commit.message:
Expand Imperator/CK3 mod detection via configurables instead of hardcoded logic (#2990)

Mod detection was entirely hardcoded (`StartsWith("The Fallen Eagle")`,
etc.) and file-tweaker blocks were gated on hardcoded boolean properties
per mod. This makes adding new mod support require C# changes. Replaces
both with configurable-driven detection.

## New configurable format

`configurables/ck3_mods.txt` and `configurables/imperator_mods.txt`
define detection rules:

```
tfe = {
    name_regex = { "^The Fallen Eagle" }
    id = { "ugc_2243307127.mod" }
}
```

A flag is activated if any loaded mod's name matches any regex **or**
its path ends with any listed ID. Both fields are optional.

## Key changes

- **`CommonUtils/ModDefinition.cs`** — New `ModDefinition` and
`ModDefinitionsReader` classes; parses the configurable format, regex
compiled with 1s timeout (ReDoS protection)
- **`configurables/ck3_mods.txt`** — Definitions for `tfe`, `wtwsms`,
`roa`, `aep`
- **`configurables/imperator_mods.txt`** — Definitions for `invictus`,
`invictus_1_7`, `terra_indomita`
- **`Configuration.cs`** — `DetectSpecificCK3Mods` now loads
`ck3_mods.txt`; `FallenEagleEnabled` etc. become computed properties
from a `HashSet<string> activeCK3ModFlags`; `GetCK3ModFlags()` builds
dynamically from loaded definitions so any new flag in the file
automatically propagates to Liquid templates.
`DetectSpecificImperatorMods` moved here from `Imperator/World.cs`;
`InvictusDetected`, `Invictus1_7Detected`, `TerraIndomitaDetected` are
now computed properties on `Configuration`; `AddImperatorModFlag()`
added for fallback detection from save data; `GetLiquidVariables()` now
includes Imperator mod flags with an `ir_` prefix (e.g. `ir_invictus`,
`ir_terra_indomita`)
- **`Imperator/World.cs`** — Calls
`config.DetectSpecificImperatorMods(UsableMods)` after mod loading;
save-data fallback detection (global flag `is_playing_invictus`, country
`unification_points` variable) handled via
`config.AddImperatorModFlag()`
- **`CK3/World.cs`** — Uses `config.InvictusDetected`,
`config.Invictus1_7Detected`, `config.TerraIndomitaDetected` instead of
reading from `irWorld`
- **`FileTweaker.cs`** — Hardcoded `if (config.FallenEagleEnabled)`
blocks replaced with a loop over `config.GetActiveCK3ModFlags()`,
loading `removable_file_blocks_{flag}.txt` /
`replaceable_file_blocks_{flag}.txt` when they exist — new mods need
only a configurable entry and the block files, no C# changes

## Backward compatibility

Existing boolean properties and flag keys (`tfe`, `vanilla`, etc.) are
preserved. `ThrowUserErrorExceptionForUnsupportedModCombinations` is
unchanged.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Expand Imperator/CK3 Mod Detection</issue_title>
> <issue_description>Original Concept:
> 
> > As far as I'm aware, the converter only really checks what Imperator
mods are being used to know what province mappings to use. While there
might not be too many cases where knowing exactly what mods were used in
Imperator would be relevant for how CK3 is setup, I figured there
*might* be a mod I couldn't think of, or one that gets made in the
future, where using it in Imperator might have you want CK3 to be
handled a little differently compared to normal (one such case might be
knowing whether the `ba-to-imperator` converter was used). So, since I
was going to mention expanding the CK3 mod liquid dictionary, I figured
I'd at least mention checking Imperator mods while I was at it. The
process regarding Imperator mods would pretty much be the same as CK3
mods below.
> > 
> > The gist of this would be probably having some configurable files
setup that list mods from Imperator and/or CK3 with ways for the
converter to recognize they are being used. For example, the converter
currently checks if TFE is being used if one of the mods chosen has its
name start with "The Fallen Eagle", which then has `tfe` be added to the
CK3 mods liquid dictionary. This could potentially be changed to
something in the configurable like:
> ```
> tfe = {
>    name = { "The Fallen Eagle" }
>    id = { ugc_2243307127.mod } # Steam Workshop ID
> }
> ```
> > I don't know what the best way to format it would be, but the idea
is that if any of the mods loaded meet either of those conditions, the
`tfe` flag gets added to the liquid dictionary, which could then be used
wherever its needed. The converter only currently checks the main
compatible, overhaul mods since those are needed to know which province
mappings will be used. But, there are potentially other mods where
knowing they will be loaded would be useful, like RICE or Cultures
Expanded, which add a lot of relevant content that might need to be
modified to better fit in a converted game. This could also be expanded
to better be used in other parts of the converter, like
`converter_cultures/faiths.txt` INVALIDATED block, the
`cultural_pillars` MOD_DEPENDENT block, or the
`removable/replaceable_file_blocks` which could be setup so that instead
of checking specifically named files, it checks if there exists a file
in the configurables folder that matches any of the CK3 mods in the mod
dictionary, like if `tfe` was in the dictionary and
`replaceable_file_blocks_tfe.txt` existed, that would be loaded, and if
`rice` was in the dictionary and `removable_file_blocks_rice.txt`
existed, that would be loaded. Overall, this would add more mod
compatibility while requiring less of the converter to be hardcoded for
specific mods.
> 
> 
> Initial Comments from IhateTrains:
> 
> > For the `name` blocks in the configurable, we could use regex, for
example `name_regex = { "^The Fallen Eagle" }` to represent "name starts
with "The Fallen Eagle" to avoid matching a "Definitely NOT The Fallen
Eagle" mod</issue_description>
> 
> <agent_instructions>Add two new configurables: `imperator_mods.txt`
and `ck3_mods.txt` with the proposed syntax. Use the Imperator ones in
the existing Imperator.World.DetectUsedMods method. Use the CK3 ones in
the existing Configuration.DetectSpecificCK3Mods method.
> Use the CK3 mod flags from the configurables in
FileTweaker.ModifyAndRemovePartsOfFiles instead of the hardcoded file
names.</agent_instructions>
> 
> ## Comments on the Issue (you are @copilot in this section)
> 
> <comments>
> </comments>
> 


</details>



<!-- START COPILOT CODING AGENT SUFFIX -->

- Fixes ParadoxGameConverters/ImperatorToCK3#2930

Add 7 CivicConnect CRM pages: dashboards, forms, tracking, admin, support

- citizen-dashboard.html: Stat cards, donut chart, complaints table, notifications, quick actions
- complaint-submission.html: Multilingual form, priority pills, voice input, drag-drop upload, SLA alerts
- complaint-tracking.html: Real-time timeline, SLA countdown, activity log, star rating, dept contact
- complaint-history.html: Filter pills, search, exportable table with pagination, summary stats
- admin-dashboard.html: 6 stat cards, 4 canvas charts (donut/line/bar/radial/heatmap), dept performance
- authority-assignment.html: Assign modal, bulk toolbar, escalation panel, NGO assignment, capacity bars
- customer-support.html: FAQ accordion, live chat widget with bot, help topics, contact section

All pages link to css/main.css, css/animations.css, js/main.js, js/charts.js
Charts auto-initialized via Charts.initAll() in charts.js
Toast notifications use CRM.Toast.* API from main.js

feat(observability): instrument core orchestration paths with OTel spans

Add OpenTelemetry spans to all 9 core orchestration services per §10.13.2:
- task.transition in TransitionService
- task.assign in SchedulerService
- worker.prepare/worker.run in WorkerSupervisorService
- worker.heartbeat in HeartbeatService
- validation.run in ValidationRunnerService
- review.route in ReviewRouterService
- review.lead_decision in ReviewDecisionService
- merge.prepare/merge.execute in MergeExecutorService

Create span name and attribute key constants in @factory/observability.
Add 14 span verification tests. Re-export SpanStatusCode, Span, and
InMemorySpanExporter from the observability package.

feat(control-plane): implement artifact and review packet retrieval endpoints

Add REST endpoints for retrieving task artifacts, review history,
review cycle packets, parsed packet content, and merge details.

Endpoints:
- GET /tasks/:taskId/artifacts - artifact tree organized by type
- GET /tasks/:taskId/packets/:packetId - parsed JSON packet content
- GET /tasks/:taskId/reviews - review cycle history with decisions
- GET /tasks/:taskId/reviews/:cycleId/packets - specialist packets
- GET /tasks/:taskId/merge - merge queue item + validation runs

New files:
- review/artifacts.controller.ts + artifacts.service.ts
- review/reviews.controller.ts + reviews.service.ts
- merge/merge-details.controller.ts + merge-details.service.ts
- 6 test files with 31 new tests (3,268 total passing)

Closes T084.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: emx-onnx-cgen
repository.description: Deterministic ONNX-to-C compiler for embedded and safety-critical systems, generating static, auditable C code with no dynamic memory or runtime dependencies.

commit.message:
Fix profile workdir: use local repo for Copilot boot, skip git ops

Copilot needs a repo with init.ps1 to emit 'Environment loaded:' —
using TEMP caused a 30min boot timeout. Now uses the local repo path
without any git fetch/checkout.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add profile to default WorkerTaskTypes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(randutil): add random value generation utilities (#607)

New package with 11 random utility functions:
- Int, Float64, Bool: basic random values
- Pick[T]: random element from slice
- Shuffle[T]: random permutation
- Sample[T]: random subset without replacement
- String, Hex, Alpha, AlphaNum: random string generators
- WeightedPick[T]: weighted random selection

All generic functions use math/rand/v2 for performance.
Includes doc.go, 25+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 3 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(application): implement audit event query service (T074)

Add AuditQueryService with getEntityTimeline() and searchAuditEvents()
for querying the append-only audit log with flexible, combinable filters
and offset-based pagination.

- New AuditEventQueryPort (read-side, CQRS-lite separation from write port)
- AuditEventFilters: entityType, entityId, eventType, actorType, actorId, startTime, endTime
- PaginatedAuditResult with total count and page metadata
- Input validation (non-empty entity params, time range consistency)
- Pagination normalization (clamping, defaults)
- Infrastructure: findByEntityPaginated() and searchFiltered() with Drizzle dynamic queries
- Infrastructure: createAuditEventQueryPortAdapter() with shared mapRowToRecord()
- 26 unit tests covering timelines, filters, pagination, validation, edge cases

commit.message:
feat: implement Azure Incident Copilot MVP

Complete TypeScript monorepo with all 87 tasks across 9 phases:

- Shared package: types, validation, SQLite persistence (node:sqlite),
  log/job/result repositories, mock AI analysis adapter, seed data
- API server: Express REST API with incident CRUD, log submission,
  job tracking, analysis results, correlation IDs, CORS
- Worker: polling-based async processor with mock AI analysis
- Frontend: React + Vite with incident list, create, detail pages,
  log submission, analysis display, job status polling
- Tests: 67 tests (unit + API integration + E2E workflow)
- Scripts: dev.sh (concurrent startup), verify.sh (E2E verification)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

plugins: add missing Zig binding types matching Python counterparts

Append types and methods to 6 Zig files to match their Python sources:

- context_base.zig: ReplyOptions, BaseContext (state, replies, middleware)
- context_tdlib.zig: InlineKeyboardButton, InlineKeyboard, ChatAction, TdlibContext
- modules.zig: ModuleState, ModuleInfo, PluginModule, ModuleManager
- router.zig: HandlerType, FilterMode, CommandHandler, MessageHandler,
  CallbackHandler, PluginRouter
- registry.zig: PluginState, PluginPriority, PluginMetaInfo, PluginStateRegistry
- loader.zig: PluginLoadError, DependencyError, ConflictError, LoadResult,
  BatchLoadResult, FullPluginLoader

All types use PascalCase, functions use camelCase.
Includes comprehensive test suites for each new type.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cond): add generic conditional and comparison utilities (#513)

New package with 10 generic utility functions:
- Ternary[T]: inline conditional expression
- Coalesce[T], CoalesceFunc[T]: first non-zero value
- Clamp[T]: restrict to range
- Min[T], Max[T]: variadic min/max
- Zero[T], IsZero[T]: zero value helpers
- Switch[T]: pattern matching with Case structs

All functions are generic and type-safe.
Includes doc.go, 25+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 4 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Each storage backend struct now has public methods matching the Python
counterpart interface:

  ConnectionErrorInfo structured error types
- All backends: Added open(), close(), get(), set(), delete(), has(), count(),
  clear(), keys(), values(), items() as public methods on the struct
- sqlite.zig, lmdb.zig: Added begin(), commit(), rollback() transaction methods
- Renamed internal vtable functions to *Impl suffix to avoid name conflicts
- Fixed var->const for test config variables (Zig 0.13 compliance)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(encoding): add base62 encode/decode utilities (#506)

Add a new encoding package with base62 encoding for compact, URL-safe
representations of binary data and integers.

New functions:
- Base62Encode — encode bytes to base62 string
- Base62Decode — decode base62 string to bytes
- Base62EncodeUint64 — encode uint64 to compact base62
- Base62DecodeUint64 — decode base62 string to uint64

Includes 14 tests, 2 benchmarks, and 2 fuzz targets.
100% statement coverage.

DevResultPacket) for inclusion in rework TaskPackets.

New files:

  review data

  at query time from already-persisted data
- services/rework-context.service.test.ts — 22 tests covering happy path,

Design: RejectionContext is derived from existing records rather than stored
redundantly, keeping data normalized and avoiding schema migrations.

feat(convert): add safe type conversion utilities (#502)

New package with 13 conversion functions:
- ToInt, ToInt64, ToFloat64, ToUint: string-to-number with fallback
- ToBool: flexible boolean parsing (true/1/yes/on)
- ToString: any-to-string conversion
- ToDuration: string-to-Duration with fallback
- MustInt, MustFloat64: panic-on-error conversions
- Ptr[T]: create pointer to value
- Deref[T], DerefOr[T]: safe pointer dereferencing

All functions return sensible defaults instead of errors.
Includes doc.go, 25+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 4 examples.

5 Zig storage backend structs (mongodb, pickledb, redis, sqlite, tinydb).

- openStorage, closeStorage: connection lifecycle

- getKeys, getValues, getItems: bulk retrieval

feat(stringutil): add string utility functions (#496)

New package with 8 string utilities:
- ToKebabCase, ToPascalCase: case conversion
- PadLeft, PadRight: string padding
- IsBlank: empty/whitespace check
- Reverse: rune-aware string reversal
- WordWrap: line wrapping at word boundaries
- CountWords: word counting

All functions are Unicode-aware (rune-based, not byte-based).
Includes doc.go, 20+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 1 fuzz, 3+ examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(maputil): add generic map utility functions (#487)

New package with 9 generic map utilities:
- Keys, Values: extract keys/values as slices
- Merge: combine multiple maps (last-write-wins)
- Pick, Omit: select/exclude specific keys
- Filter: predicate-based filtering
- MapValues: transform values preserving keys
- Invert: swap keys and values
- Equal: deep equality check

All functions are nil-safe and non-mutating.
Includes doc.go, 22 unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 4 examples.
README updated with package entry.

Sync with copilot-sdk: ignore cliPath when cliUrl is set (#44)

When cli_url is provided, cliPath should not be resolved.
The Laravel SDK already handles this correctly via TCP mode, but
now ProcessManager throws a descriptive error when the CLI binary
cannot be found, matching the official SDK behavior.

Official SDK: ab8cc5a [nodejs] Ignore `cliPath` if `cliUrl` is set (#787)

commit.message:
Add high-level Zig bindings matching Python media API

Add subprocess-based types to the media module Zig bindings that
mirror the Python bindings API:

- image.zig: ResizeMode, FilterType, ImageFormat enums and
  ImageProcessor struct (init, resize, crop, rotate, applyFilter,
  getInfo, convertFormat) using ImageMagick/FFmpeg
- video.zig: ProcessorVideoInfo with toJson, VideoProcessor struct
  (init, getInfo, getDuration, getResolution, getCodec, extractFrame,
  trim, videoResize, convertFormat, extractAudio), and toJson on
  existing VideoInfo
- audio.zig: ProcessorAudioInfo with toJson, AudioProcessor struct
  (init, getInfo, convert, trim, merge, changeVolume, extractSegment,
  getDuration, getFormat), and toJson on existing AudioInfo
- thumbnail.zig: CropStrategy enum and ThumbnailGenerator struct
  (init, generateImageThumbnail, generateVideoThumbnail,
  generateThumbnailGrid, generateAnimatedThumbnail)

All implementations use std.process.Child to spawn ffmpeg/imagemagick
commands, matching the Python subprocess-based approach.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- hash.zig: md5, sha1, sha256, sha512 (raw, hex, file variants)

  case conversion, slugify, startsWith/endsWith/contains
- time.zig: Duration/Time structs, now, monotonic, formatTime,
  ISO 8601 parse/format, humanize, parseDuration, sleep
- cache.zig: has, delete, clear, count methods + Cache alias
- versions.zig: formatted, fullInfo, checkCompatibility, getInfo
- message_utils.zig: stripMarkdown
- git.zig: gitFetch, gitCheckout, gitRebase, getCommitHash,
  GitHubAPI struct with releases/download methods
- decorators.zig: typingAction, logCommand, decorator builders

commit.message:
feat(rust): reach 100% Python parity for config bindings

Add missing types, traits, and functions to match all Python modules:

Constants (constants.py):
- APP_NAME, APP_VERSION, APP_AUTHOR, BOT_PREFIX, BOT_MAX_COMMANDS

Env (env.py):
- Env::get_float, Env::get_env_list
- set_env(), unset_env()

Config (config.py):
- Error types: ConfigError, ValidationError
- Enums: AuthMode, LogLevel
- Structs: DatabaseConfig, ProxyConfig, ZsysConfig (full Config dataclass)
- ZsysConfig methods: to_dict/from_dict, to_json/from_json, save/load,
  get/set, merge, validate/is_valid
- ZsysConfigBuilder with full fluent API (30+ builder methods)
- ConfigValidator with validate() and validate_strict()
- Preset factories: default_config, bot_config, user_config,
  minimal_config, test_config
- Top-level loaders: config_from_file, config_from_env, config_from_dict

Dependencies: added serde + serde_json to Cargo.toml

fix(frontend): ring text — fix stale texture, animate % on load

- Remove throttle: text texture updates every frame so % counts up
  0→100 in sync with the ring arc lerp animation on page load

- Restore TEXTURE0 after binding TEXTURE4 to prevent glyph atlas
  from leaking into the ring text sampler

  data change

repository.name: trex

commit.message:
fix: boolean condition for full_rescrape + 15min timeout on simulate

- full_rescrape conditions used string 'true' but input is type:boolean;
  fix to compare against boolean true so the correct scrape step runs
- add timeout-minutes: 15 to both simulate steps to prevent silent hangs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(frontend): ring text Orbitron font + show %/use theme font settings

- Default font: Orbitron (slick HUD look for percentage display)
- 'Show %' toggle (default: on) — hides/shows the centered percentage
- 'Use Theme Font' toggle (default: off) — switches to terminal monospace
- Both settings trigger immediate text re-render (bypass throttle)
- Empty texture uploaded when % disabled (no stale text)

commit.message:
refactor: move all C code from bindings/c/ → c/

C implementations are source code, not bindings.
Structure: zsys/{module}/c/ = C source, zsys/{module}/bindings/ = language bindings only.

378 files changed — all bindings/c/ content merged into {module}/c/
Removed zsys/bindings/ top-level directory

Complete Zig port of Python crypto licensing modules.

feat: complete V bindings for plugins, api, and control modules

Add missing functions/structs/methods to achieve Python parity:

Plugins module:
- router.v: HandlerType/FilterMode enums, CommandHandler/MessageHandler/
  CallbackHandler structs, Command.matches()/full_usage()/to_map(),
  Router rate limiting, sub-routers, message/callback handlers,
  enable/disable, owner/admin management, help text generation, reset
- loader.v: LoadResult/BatchLoadResult structs, plugin dir management,
  reload/unload_all/reload_all, init/cleanup hooks, load stats, reset
- registry.v: PluginState/PluginPriority enums, state management,
  enable/disable, config storage, lifecycle hooks, dependency resolution,
  load ordering, stats
- watcher.v: WatchEventType enum, WatchEvent/WatcherConfig structs,
  directory management, event handlers, check() for manual polling,
  file tracking, stats, reset
- context_base.v: ReplyOptions struct, full BaseContext with state
  management, reply queuing (text/photo/document/sticker/callback/
  edit/delete/forward), middleware data, raw update storage
- context_tdlib.v: InlineKeyboardButton/InlineKeyboard/ChatAction
  structs, sender/chat/message/media info setters, reply methods
  (video/audio/voice/location/contact), admin actions (pin/ban/
  unban/promote), chat actions, serialization
- modules.v: ModuleState enum, ModuleInfo struct, PluginModule with
  full lifecycle (init/start/stop), command/handler/cleanup management,
  ModuleManager with dependency resolution, batch lifecycle ops
- examples: Enhanced help/ping/say with module list, pong, code
  formatting, reply mode

API module:
- api_adapter.v: RouteHandler type, AdapterRoute struct, APIRouteAdapter
  with register_route/handle_request/get_routes/remove_route
- base.v: Route struct, BaseRouter with prefix-based grouping and
  HTTP method helpers (get/post/put/delete)

Control module:
- control.v: ControlConfig with owner/allowed users/validation,
  BotControl with start/stop/restart, module load/unload/reload,
  status/uptime, command registration/execution, owner command handling
